# Rwanda Plugs

> Complete reference for electrical plug and socket types used in Rwanda.

Rwanda uses plug types C, E, and G at 230V / 50Hz.

## Plug Types

- Type C (Europlug): Two-pin ungrounded plug, 4.0 mm diameter pins. Compatible with Type E sockets. No dedicated Type C sockets exist in Rwanda — Type C plugs are used everywhere via compatibility with Type E sockets.
- Type E (French): Two-pin grounded plug with ground pin on the socket, 4.8 mm diameter pins. Historical standard from Belgian colonial infrastructure. Most widespread grounded socket in older buildings.
- Type G (British BS 1363): Three rectangular pins in triangular pattern, fused at 13 A. Official East African Community (EAC) standard, required in all new construction since the early 2010s.

## Key Facts

- Voltage: 230 V
- Frequency: 50 Hz
- Compatible with most of Europe and Africa
- EAC standard (Type G) shared with Kenya, Tanzania, and Uganda

## Type J Database Error

Some international databases, notably those derived from IEC data, incorrectly listed Rwanda as using plug types C and J. Type J is the Swiss plug, used exclusively in Switzerland and Liechtenstein. This was a data entry error that propagated across reference databases. The IEC corrected Rwanda's listing in 2025 to types C, E, F, and G, removing the erroneous Type J.

## Travel Advice

- European two-pin (Type C) plugs work directly in most Rwandan sockets
- French (Type E) plugs work in older buildings
- UK, US, and Australian travellers need an adapter
- A universal travel adapter is recommended for best coverage

## Sources

- IEC World Plugs (iec.ch/world-plugs) — lists C, E, F, G
- worldstandards.eu — lists C, E, F, G
- Wikipedia: Energy in Rwanda — lists C, E, G
- Wikivoyage: Rwanda — lists C, E, G
- Website: https://plugs.rw
